reviews.author: Jonah Jagadeshan
reviews.text: If you’re in doubt, just make sure you go to this one. This is the best beach in Phu Quoc, waters are not choppy and well maintained beach. Drinks are not cheap, maybe that’s the reason there are not too many people and it’s maintained clean.

reviews.author: Ms. Soca
reviews.text: Perfect beach to relax. There a bunch of beach clubs that cost approx 150k per chair or you use your towel lay somewhere. Not overly crowded. There’s parking right next to the beach. 10/10

reviews.author: Rawa
reviews.text: Nice beach and the white sand is amazing. The water is not so nice, it’s green, not clear at all, and sadly so much plastic everywhere, I don’t know how much I picked up, it’s so sad that we destroy our world like this, if you leave your shit on the beach or in the water you should get fined, high amount and people will maybe stop.

reviews.author: Robert Skovran
reviews.text: Long and clean beach with beautifully white sand. It is relatively more crowded compared to our experience at Bai Dai Beach in the north. Sunbeds are also more expensive here – at Khem Beach it costs 150,000 VND per bed for 4 hours, while at Bai Dai Beach it is 50,000 VND per bed with no time limit. After visiting several beaches, we personally prefer the northern part of the island. Another advantage is that you can easily reach the north by public transportation (bus).

reviews.author: Gabriela Zeveleanu
reviews.text: Very nice and beautiful beach! When we were here, it was very clean and comfy. A bit crowded for my taste, but, anyway, the view, the atmosphere, the water, everything was amazing!

reviews.author: Valentin L
reviews.text: Beautiful beach! The place was very clean and the palm trees provided some nice natural shade. The price of the sunbeds were 2.5 euros for 4 hours. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Mays Zea
reviews.text: The beach is beautiful, with shady coconut trees and fine white sand. There are lifeguards, so it's very safe. Come here early in the morning to watch the sunrise.

reviews.author: Pavel Verpakhovskiy
reviews.text: Khem Beach is truly a breathtaking spot! The sand is impeccably white and soft underfoot, while the water boasts incredible clarity and a mesmerizing turquoise hue. One of the best perks? Sunbeds are provided free of charge — but act fast, as they tend to fill up quickly by 10 am. The area is well‑served by numerous cafés offering a variety of refreshments and light meals, so you’ll never go hungry or thirsty. What really sets this beach apart, though, are its stunning views — picture‑perfect scenery that makes every moment here feel like a postcard come to life. A few tips: Arrive before 8 am for a peaceful, crowd‑free experience. Don’t forget sun protection — the tropical sun gets strong by mid‑morning. Explore a bit further along the shore if you’re seeking a quieter spot. Overall, Khem Beach delivers an unforgettable blend of natural beauty, convenience, and relaxation. A must‑visit for any traveler to Phu Quoc!

reviews.author: philip hollin
reviews.text: A beach and sea full of trash (mainly plastic) Nice just where the bars are Well done the 2 ladies collecting rubbish in the sea and the workers trying to collect it all but the amount of trash is unbelievable. Also at the bottom end of the beach, Marriot hotels seem to think it owns the beach and has guards placed to stop you.

reviews.author: Zygzac
reviews.text: You can find plenty beach bars and restaurants here. Beach is quite long but not too wide and there is less space for finding your own spot. You can encounter some rubbish as well. Pretty views with palms.Good for drinking but not for swimming and enjoying the beach.

reviews.author: Sahar Ardibili
reviews.text: A beautiful beach with soft white sand and a relaxed, uncrowded feel. We stayed near Khem Beach between December 23–25 2025, and returned again on the 30th before leaving the island. While it was busier later on, the atmosphere and setting were just as enjoyable, making it our favorite beach throughout our stay. We also stayed at a resort near Olong Beach and made a stop at Sao beach.

reviews.author: Roger
reviews.text: The beach itself is extremely beautiful, lined with palm trees that provide natural shade, making it possible to relax without renting a sunbed. The water and sand are truly nice and, in terms of natural beauty, it’s probably the nicest beach on Phu Quoc and easily deserving of five stars. Unfortunately, it was already very crowded, even in the morning, which detracts somewhat from the experience. There are a few restaurants along the promenade above the beach; we tried one, but apart from the location, it wasn’t anything special and was rather expensive. On the positive side, the area felt very clean, and scooter parking was easy and convenient.

reviews.author: Sasa Zagorc
reviews.text: A long and beautiful sandy beach in the SE part of the island, which might be heavily exposed to the rubbish from the open seas. The beach workers are cleaning it constantly. In normal circumstances, the beach offers a relaxing and soothing experience with lots of shade from the palm trees. With shallow waters, it seems a perfect place for families with small kids. There are plenty of bars and restaurants. Access to the beach is simple.

reviews.author: Maria
reviews.text: If this is the best Phu Quoc beach, then there is no reason to come the island at all. All full of paid sunbeds (150K vnd for 3-4 hours and each adult must take sunbed. In some places they also force you to buy a drink for minimum 100K each person) Construction works and noises all over. Dirty polluted sea. Today tourists were cleaning it from plastic as it is impossible to swim in this trash bin. No go Phu quoc as such is a disappointment.

reviews.author: Bogdan Bogdan
reviews.text: Nice beach, nice sand, good vibes and also good restaurants on beach. If you dont have a beach towel you can rent on the entrance, or at Herring Bar. You can also lay on sunbeds if you buy food or drinks from beach bars

reviews.author: Hồ Lãnh Minh
reviews.text: Best beach on the island. The beach and surrounding area is immaculate! All very positive other than the tourists that visit this place.

reviews.author: Denis Poruban
reviews.text: A very wide sandy beach with a gentle entry into the sea. There are plenty of options for food and drinks. The beach is very clean, as cleaners can be seen regularly maintaining it. I definitely recommend visiting.

reviews.author: Gia Bảo Hoàng
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful beaches in Phu Quoc, offering a pristine shoreline and breathtaking sunrise views

reviews.author: An Hoe
reviews.text: The beach itself is beautiful with long white sand and truly crystal clear water but too shallow and packed with too many people, children... No offense but suitable for families or Chinese, Vietnamese, Indians, Russians... And also a soulless residence :)

reviews.author: SB
reviews.text: Easily the best beach in Phu Quoc along with Sao Beach but it’s really not all that great. Really don’t understand the attraction of this overrated island

reviews.author: Edoardo Nerenzi
reviews.text: Could be a nice beach if only it wasn't for the huge amount of waste. I've never seen a beach/sea with so much trash on it. My friends and I spent one hour cleaning up the sea and lots of other tourists joined in too. We filled up dozens of baskets with plastic. It's crazy how many tourists were ignoring this problem! Most tourists are crammed in the small section of the beach without trash, the rest of the beach is a landfill

reviews.author: allPhoto Paul
reviews.text: This is in my opinion one of the best beaches on the island. White sand, lots of palm trees and few beach clubs where u can rent beds and have food. Recommended

reviews.author: V -
reviews.text: The most beautiful beach on the island, if not in the world! It’s an incredibly relaxing place to spend time: plenty of shaded spots, crystal-clear water, and a calm, welcoming atmosphere. A big compliment to the staff, who work continuously to keep the beach clean it’s truly impressive. This is now my favorite beach in Phu Quoc. Just one note: unlike Sao Beach, there are no small shops where you can buy snacks or drinks. Only restaurants are available, and prices are naturally a bit higher.

reviews.author: Time2bookatrip. com
reviews.text: It’s probably the best beach on Island the water is clear blue a bit wavy but perfect to spend your day. There are a lot of palm trees, white sand, and bars where you can take a sunbed or just come with your towel and sit on the sand. Their bathroom and changing room that we need to pay 10,000 dong. Lifeguard are here. Finally, it can be crowded sometimes, but when you come in the morning, it is a perfect spot.

reviews.author: pleurat asllani
reviews.text: A great destination for those who seek a white sand beach with choices of beach bars, clean amenities and superb services. You can find privacy in the shadow of palm trees, enjoy coconuts and count the waves. Prices are reasonable: - 100 / 200k VND per sunbed. - 50 / 90 k VND per coffee, coconut or beer. - 100 / 150k VND for cocktails - Approx. 250k VND per meal plate.

reviews.author: Victorya
reviews.text: The sand is beautiful white and the water has a perfect turquoise color. Unfortunately in certain areas due to the current there is a bit of trash in the water. If you want a sun bed make sure to get there early as from what I saw there aren't a lot of places that offer them and they fill up very quick. They cost 150k and if you want a towel that's 20k extra. If you walk for a bit it becomes less crowded but by the area of the sun beds it is very crowded. It almost feels like a not so secret "secret" beach. Still absolutely recommend it though!!

reviews.author: Ivan George
reviews.text: You only realize the scale of the problem when you witness these scenes firsthand. It was heartbreaking to watch this paradise slowly fill up with waste coming in from the sea. No more than ten employees were trying to pick up the debris by hand. Gradually, some tourists joined the group and lent a hand. Coming from Europe, it is very hard for me to understand how the local population in a paradise like this doesn't protect the environment at all. Trash is everywhere, even in the sea. It’s a shame that the Vietnamese don’t know how to protect the paradise they live in.

reviews.author: Alexandrescu Mihai
reviews.text: The most beautiful beach in Phu QUOC and in my opinion, one of the most beautiful beaches in the world, on the same level with White Beach in Boracay and other pristine beaches. The beach is pristine, ultra clean with white sand and hundreds of coconut trees everywhere, heaven on earth.

reviews.author: Fellow Traveler From Canada
reviews.text: We visited during Tet, and it was absolutely beautiful—truly stunning. The palm trees lining the beach provide abundant shade, and there are plenty of rental chairs available for relaxing by the water. We paid 315,000 VND for two comfortable chairs in the An area, which felt reasonable for the setting and convenience. While we enjoyed being served directly at our chairs, the coffee wasn’t great, so I’d recommend skipping the food and drinks and simply soaking in the surroundings instead. The beach itself is amazing—clean, scenic, and the perfect place to unwind.

reviews.author: Irek Irek
reviews.text: Dirty beach with high prices. You pay for sunbeds, toilets, shower. The restaurants on the beach are expensive and charge 5% servise fee. There are much better beaches at Phu Quoc. When they try to clean the beach they use a tractor during peak time. The driver drove fast and even honk on people! Not recommend to visit.
